title: centroplacaceae
intro: The Centropogonaceae, also spelled as Centroplacaceae, is a family of flowering plants. It is a group of monocotyledons that primarily consist of tropical plants, including shrubs, vines, and herbs. These plants are known for their small white or purple flowers and are mostly found in Central and South America, as well as parts of the Caribbean. The family Centropogonaceae is considered to be part of the larger order Zingiberales, which also includes the banana and ginger families.
